Industry Research
Section 02

The Problem

The industry keeps building better detection. But a layer is missing.

The Problem

The Industry Is Solving the Wrong Problem

We will make detection 10x better. But it won't solve the problem. We need judgment.

hearing_disabled

Detection without understanding is just noise

Your security stack can flag a file download in milliseconds. But it can't tell you whether that action was a mistake, a workaround, or a genuine threat.

rule

Policies don't prevent. They generate tickets.

Every new rule creates a new category of alerts. Most are false positives. Your analysts know it. Your employees know it.

speed

Speed to alert, not speed to resolution

The average insider incident costs $16.2M and takes nearly three months to contain. Tools surface volume, not clarity.

Security teams are drowning in alerts while real insider risks slip through. They need decisions, not more noise.
The Problem

The industry keeps building better locks. The problem was never the locks.

They all assume more enforcement equals more security.

1

More pattern matching, applied faster

Legacy approaches treat insider risk like a keyword search problem. False positive rate is 90%, endless deployments.

2

Data lineage is just movement tracking without meaning

A new generation of tools can tell you exactly where a file went. What they can't tell you is whether it mattered.

3

Network-first thinking in a people-first problem

Cloud security platforms were built to protect perimeters. Insider risk isn't a network problem. It's a judgment problem.

4

Real-time blocking, marketed as intelligence

Faster blocking is still just blocking. It still doesn't explain why something was risky. It still doesn't engage the person involved.

Section 03

The Axia Approach

Defining a New Category in Data Security

The Approach

Introducing

Axia

The AI-powered judgment layer that connects to your existing tools, understands what DLP can't, and turns data into decisions.

Organizational Memory
Axia builds contextual baselines over time: what's normal for a role, typical collaboration patterns for teams, and deviations that matter. Every week it runs, it becomes smarter.

Legacy DLP

Competitive

Why Axia Wins

Legacy approaches vs. the Axia way.

Legacy Approach Axia Approach
Detect — Pattern matching finds content → Understand — Vertical AI comprehends intent and context
Block — Automated enforcement stops actions → Decide — Judgment determines appropriate response
Alert — Notifications flood the SOC → Engage — Orchestrated conversations with the right people
vs. Traditional DLP
Palo Alto, Forcepoint, Purview
Axia adds semantic understanding, vertical AI, and active engagement. No risk. No rip and replace. 3-hour deployment.
vs. DSPM
Cyera, Sentra
DSPM protects data at rest. Axia protects data in motion through people. Different layer, complementary value.
vs. AI-Native DLP
MIND, Orion, CyberHaven
Axia adds human engagement and intent understanding. MIND automates blocking; Axia judges and engages humans in decisions.

Go-To-Market

Why Axia's GTM Wins

The market is stuck. Incumbents are complex. Startups can't displace them. Axia goes around both.

High Risk GTM

Axia

Judgment layer on top of existing DLP. No rip-and-replace. Enhances what's already there. AI-native from day one.

Layer, Don't Replace
